title: ferrotorch-core: RfftBackward / IrfftBackward / HfftBackward / IhfftBackward / RfftnBackward / IrfftnBackward missing N normalization scale — surfaced by phase 2.7 (#769)
description:
PyTorch's grad of rfft (and the rest of the real-only / Hermitian-only FFT ops) for a sum-of-real-plus-imag scalar loss differs from ferrotorch's by a multiplicative factor.

Concretely, for x = arange(1..=8) f64 and L = sum(real(rfft(x))) + sum(imag(rfft(x))),
PyTorch grad: [5, -2.4142, 1, -0.4142, 1, 0.4142, 1, 2.4142]
ferrotorch grad (RfftBackward): [1, -0.6036, 0, -0.1036, 0, 0.1036, 0, 0.6036]
Ratio: 5x = N/2 + 1 (the rfft K) — ferrotorch's irfft normalizes by 1/N which is the correct *forward* irfft semantic but the wrong VJP.

Per torch.autograd FftR2CBackward, the correct VJP is unnormalized irfft (i.e. ferrotorch::fft::irfft(...) * N).
Same pattern for IrfftBackward (rfft-of-grad needs *N), HfftBackward / IhfftBackward (similar mirror).

Affected fixtures: cpu_rfft_differentiable, cpu_irfft_differentiable, cpu_rfftn_differentiable, cpu_irfftn_differentiable, cpu_hfft_differentiable.
Mitigation in phase 2.7 conformance: cascade_skip applied for these tag families.
